extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Proof by invariant that the conjunction (opted_out AND sent) is never reachable. Initial state: opted_out=false, sent=false, so the conjunction is false. Examine every transition for preservation: (1) t_optout sets opted_out:=true AND sent:=false atomically, so the resulting state has sent=false and the implication holds. (2) t_send_allowed is the ONLY transition that sets sent:=true, and its guard is `not opted_out`; thus whenever sent becomes true, opted_out is false in the same state, making the antecedent false. (3) t_send_blocked fires only when opted_out is true and sets sent:=false, so the consequent (not sent) holds. (4) t_tick sets sent:=false unconditionally, so the consequent holds regardless of opted_out. There is no transition that sets opted_out:=true without simultaneously clearing sent, and none that sets sent:=true while opted_out is true. Therefore opted_out AND sent is unreachable, the implication holds in every reachable state, and the property is satisfied on all executions (the 5-event bound is irrelevant since the invariant is preserved by every step). No counterexample exists.
